(description edited by @lornajane as the idea takes shape!)
The goal is to add linting support for AsyncAPI files to Redocly. Initially this will be partial AsyncAPI v2.6.0 support, and feedback is very welcome.
Examples of AsyncAPI files can be found in the AsyncAPI project: https://github.com/asyncapi/spec/tree/master/examples
- [ ] All new/updated code is covered with tests
- [x] Security impact of change has been considered
- [x] Code follows company security practices and guidelines

When adding rules, we have to export them from [index files](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-f277132551c50a77a6ebd53914aa50dc43d628d8675938b5769cc7a3eab1f0c1R56), add into our [predefined rulesets](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-f197d2a38d8cd9f2d09464deb9747226062f3ad06e30c0e77fcb6f8138f31719R3), and list in the [config validation file (redocly-yaml.ts)](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-2528117ea3cbe25dfbfd0583c9b11ba7baa7ef347184dfba32a73ec486aa1a5e). Currently they are not completely in sync with each other. Introducing strict typing helps to fix the discrepancies.
However, TS v4 works inconsistently, so I updated it to v5. This caused update/installation of several other packages (including Jest and ESLint).

Most of the updated test files are merely a clean-up after upgrading to Jest v29. Exceptions are: [load.test.ts](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-79f56024a65109d10c766475e388ab5e6f998bc4a3697af2f03a9dcf0f8a6a56R175), [config-resolvers.test.ts.snap](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-d32d701bdd9d26b88498888c8c1c010798b3f526f1ee1609a4d44eb9fd2dcf99R1) and [this snapshot](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-d07667a6aabcd49c1059ea8906e66de4fee9049d6e308d25d0750afbd8ed99b8).

- ~Should the previously omitted rules use severity '**off**' or '**error**' in the [**all**](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-b4ab3024ec27a1473c25552e3747d9a56224631682812b8bdd180c3affeb0c74R42) ruleset?~ -> 'error'

- Are we going to create a separate **async2** section in our rulesets like we have for exclusive OAS2/3 rules? (See [here](https://github.com/Redocly/redocly-cli/pull/1308/files#diff-f197d2a38d8cd9f2d09464deb9747226062f3ad06e30c0e77fcb6f8138f31719R3)). cc @lornajane

I would like to introduce global execution timeout timer, that will help to prevent infinity execution of some Arazzo files that can be looped with allowed `goto` and `workflowId` syntax.
- We will start Timer before we walk through the list of all Arazzo files we need to execute within this Respect run session
- Calculation decision is made on difference of RESPECT_TIMEOUT and elapsedTime
- There is one actual place where timer check done, all other places in code operate on its result:
-- on `Step` level. In this case we stop other steps execution and report error
This will affect `json-output` structure by adding additional property `globalTimeoutError` [boolean] on two levels:
- per file log level, that indicate that some timeout was hit or not during  that file execution
- per workflow log level, that indicate that timeout was hit or not in workflow during its steps run.

Introduced new `respect` command cli and `respect-core` `run()` options `no-secrets-masking` and `noSecretsMasking`.

This new option disables masking of secrets in the output. By default, any sensitive information, such as values described with `format: password`, as well as tokens and authentication headers from `x-security`,are masked with `********` in both terminal logs and file outputs. When this flag is set to `true`,  the raw (unmasked) data shows in all outputs.
So in practice, when `noSecretsMasking ` is provided and is `true`:
- Terminal output will not mask secrets with `********`.
- `run` function execution result will contain additional property, called `secretValues` with the list of secret strings, it will be the consumer's responsibility to mask them.
- `har-output` will not mask secrets with `********` inside `entries`.

- `json-output` will not mask secrets with `********` inside workflow and step data + will contain additional property, called `secretValues` with the list of secret strings, it will be the consumer's responsibility to mask them:

Tests relying on previous behavior started failing:

* constructor mocks must use real functions/classes (replaced arrow functions with function/class declarations).

In OAS 3.1's JSON Schema, `maximum` and `exclusiveMaximum` are both numbers, so specifying both is redundant and likely a mistake. Same applies to `minimum` and `exclusiveMinimum`.
This PR adds a new lint rule `no-mixed-maximum-and-exclusive-maximum` that warns when these keywords are used together in the same schema.
- Configured as `off` in `spec` preset (default), `warn` in `recommended-strict` preset

- 5 unit tests covering: maximum+exclusiveMaximum, minimum+exclusiveMinimum, only-maximum, only-exclusiveMaximum, both pairs in same schema
